name: Real-world TPE Alzheimer cohort reported slower cognitive decline against historical controls
summary: A real-world Alzheimer disease cohort reported slower MMSE and memory decline after repeated TPE compared with matched historical controls, with no severe adverse events reported.
statement: In 32 treated mild-to-moderate Alzheimer disease patients compared with 194 historical controls, repeated TPE was associated with slower decline in MMSE and memory measures, while functional FAQ differences were not statistically significant.
time_horizon: Six weekly intensive sessions followed by at least ten monthly maintenance sessions
quantitative_note: The paper reports 514 procedures, no severe adverse events, about 45% less MMSE decline versus historical controls, and nonsignificant FAQ functional difference.
caveats: The comparator was historical rather than randomized contemporaneous control.
Selection bias, treatment cost, medication differences, education differences, and disease context limit generalization.
This supports disease-boundary context for Alzheimer disease, not broad healthy-aging benefit.
